mnemonics: REDUCE (Refuse, Reduce, Reuse, Recycle, Recover, Dispose) for e-waste management hierarchy
Toxic E-Waste Leaches (TEWL) for remembering common hazardous components: Toxins (like lead), Electronics, Waste, Leaching into soil/water
keyPoints: Understand the environmental and health hazards of improper e-waste disposal, including soil and water contamination.
Identify the key stages of the e-waste management hierarchy: Refuse, Reduce, Reuse, Recycle, Recover, and responsible Disposal.
Explain the importance of formal recycling processes and legislation (like EPR - Extended Producer Responsibility) in managing e-waste.
commonMistakes: Assuming throwing e-waste in general trash is harmless because it's 'just a phone'.
Believing that all recycling is equally effective, without distinguishing between formal/informal sectors.
Overlooking the 'Refuse' and 'Reduce' steps of the hierarchy and focusing only on recycling.
